claims.statement: ER=EPR motivates interpreting certain maximally entangled black-hole states as Einstein-Rosen bridge states.

validityDomain.system: entangled black holes and holographic quantum gravity context
constraints: applies only to ER=EPR research interpretation of entangled black-hole states
does not imply generic real-universe wormhole inventory
does not provide propulsion, metric-equivalence, or local engineering evidence

sources.citation: Maldacena, J. and Susskind, L. (2013). Cool horizons for entangled black holes. Fortschritte der Physik. arXiv:1306.0533.
sources.note: Introduces the ER=EPR conjectural context used by this Stage 1 proxy lane.

claims.notes: Research context only; no CL0-CL4 promotion.

claims.statement: In AdS/CFT contexts, entanglement entropy can be related to minimal-surface area, motivating A_info-style proxy bookkeeping.

constraints: area/entropy bookkeeping is valid only inside holographic proxy contexts
does not apply to arbitrary spacetime geometries
does not certify metric, stress-energy, or wormhole-count claims

sources.citation: Ryu, S. and Takayanagi, T. (2006). Holographic derivation of entanglement entropy from AdS/CFT. Physical Review Letters. arXiv:hep-th/0603001.
sources.note: Primary source for holographic entropy-area relation motivating proxy bookkeeping.

claims.statement: Entanglement changes can correspond to changes in spacetime connectedness in holographic quantum-gravity reasoning.

constraints: used as interpretive context for entanglement/connectivity reasoning
does not establish real-universe bridge inventory
does not replace metric, ADM, geometry, stress-energy, or guardrail congruence

sources.citation: Van Raamsdonk, M. (2010). Building up spacetime with quantum entanglement. General Relativity and Gravitation. arXiv:1005.3035.

claims.statement: In a two-boundary eternal BTZ setup, a double-trace interaction can produce negative average null energy whose backreaction makes the ER bridge traversable without enabling causality violation.

validityDomain.system: specific holographic two-boundary traversable-wormhole model family
constraints: requires the double-trace traversability setup and model assumptions
not a general local engineering mechanism
does not provide propulsion, energy-source, or CL0-CL4 promotion evidence

sources.citation: Gao, P., Jafferis, D. L. and Wall, A. C. (2017). Traversable wormholes via a double trace deformation. Journal of High Energy Physics. arXiv:1608.05687.
sources.note: Specific reduced-order model motivating the correct-sign double-trace gate.

claims.statement: The 2022 quantum-processor experiment is a precedent for simulating traversable-wormhole-like dynamics in a sparsified SYK-like model.

validityDomain.system: small quantum processor and sparsified SYK-like toy model
constraints: simulation precedent only
not proof of real-universe wormholes
must be interpreted with control and critique guardrails

sources.citation: Jafferis, D. et al. (2022). Traversable wormhole dynamics on a quantum processor. Nature 612, 51-55.
sources.note: Processor simulation precedent for toy-model dynamics.

claims.statement: Published criticism of the 2022 experiment argues that small commuting models may lack gravitational features, making thermalization, scrambling, noncommuting controls, and control leakage checks mandatory.

validityDomain.system: guardrail for interpreting small toy simulations
constraints: requires explicit thermalization and scrambling diagnostics
requires noncommuting/control-leakage checks
prevents toy-simulation results from being treated as settled gravitational evidence

sources.citation: Kobrin, B. et al. (2025). Comment on traversable wormhole dynamics on a quantum processor. Nature. DOI:10.1038/s41586-025-08939-7.
sources.note: Matters Arising critique motivating guardrail diagnostics.

sources.citation: Jafferis, D. et al. (2025). Reply to comment on traversable wormhole dynamics on a quantum processor. Nature. DOI:10.1038/s41586-025-08995-z.
sources.note: Reply documenting that interpretation remains actively contested.

claims.notes: Guardrail claim, not negative proof of all toy-model value.

claims.statement: High entropy can mask quantum effects, motivating lambda_S = exp(deltaS_eff) and quantum-visibility demotion.

constraints: lambda_S and hbar_eff/hbar are diagnostic bookkeeping variables
hbar is not physically changed
entropy stretch can demote visible proxy support but cannot promote CL0-CL4 evidence

sources.citation: Zurek, W. H. (2003). Decoherence, einselection, and the quantum origins of the classical. Reviews of Modern Physics. arXiv:quant-ph/0306072.
sources.note: Context for environmental decoherence and reduced visibility of quantum effects.

sources.citation: Schlosshauer, M. (2005). Decoherence, the measurement problem, and interpretations of quantum mechanics. Reviews of Modern Physics. arXiv:quant-ph/0312059.
sources.note: Review context for decoherence masking quantum coherence in effective descriptions.

claims.notes: Diagnostic demotion only; no physical hbar mutation.

claims.statement: StarSim / Gaia-like astrometric structure may be used as ordinary cosmological structure prior, not direct ER=EPR evidence.

validityDomain.system: observational astrophysics prior and null-model context
constraints: astrometric or stellar-activity structure may supply ordinary astrophysical priors
does not count as direct ER=EPR evidence
does not support local wormhole density, propulsion, or photon-production claims

sources.citation: Herrero, E. et al. (2016). Modelling the photosphere of active stars for planet detection and characterization. Astronomy & Astrophysics. arXiv:1511.06717.
sources.equation: StarSim astrometry is not direct ER=EPR evidence
sources.note: StarSim modelling context for stellar activity and ordinary astrophysical signal simulation.

sources.citation: Eriksson, U. et al. (2018). Spotting stellar activity cycles in Gaia astrometry. Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society. arXiv:1802.09943.
sources.note: Gaia astrometry context for stellar activity signals as observational astrophysics, not ER=EPR evidence.

sources.citation: Gaia Collaboration et al. (2016). The Gaia mission. Astronomy & Astrophysics 595, A1. arXiv:1609.04153.
sources.note: Gaia mission reference for astrometric structure data context.

claims.notes: Astrometry remains a prior/null-model source only.
